items.text:
QUEIROZ IS GONE. WHO SHOULD LEAD THE BLACK STARS NEXT? 🇬🇭

Carlos Queiroz has resigned as Black Stars head coach, two days after Ghana's World Cup exit to Colombia.

In his farewell message, the Portuguese coach said football teaches you that "You either win or you learn" — and left a parting warning: success must start off the field, with better structures to prepare and protect Ghana's talent.

He leaves after taking Ghana to the knockout rounds of a World Cup — a win over Panama, that famous goalless draw with England, and a nation daring to dream again.

The GFA must now find a new leader before the AFCON qualifiers. Reports name Hervé Renard among the favourites — nothing is confirmed.

(Source: GhanaWeb | GBC | Graphic Online)
#GreaterNews #Ghana #BlackStars #Queiroz #GhanaFootball

Be honest — who do YOU want in the dugout next? 👇

Carlos Queiroz has resigned as Black Stars coach, two days after the World Cup exit to Colombia. His parting message: "You either win or you learn." GFA now hunts a new coach before AFCON qualifiers. (Source: GhanaWeb/GBC) #GreaterNews #BlackStars

GHANA JUST PAID $700 MILLION OF ITS DEBT — EARLY.

The Ministry of Finance has fully settled a US$700 million Eurobond obligation ahead of schedule: $525.2 million in principal and $174.8 million in interest.

That brings Ghana's total payments to Eurobond holders since January 2025 to US$2.1 billion — all under the debt exchange programme.

The Ministry says the payment was made without straining the country's foreign reserves.

(Source: Ministry of Finance | GBC | The Herald)
#GreaterNews #Ghana #GhanaEconomy #Eurobond #Debt

Do you feel the economic recovery in your daily life yet? Talk to us. 💬

Ghana has settled a $700m Eurobond ahead of schedule — $525.2m principal, $174.8m interest — bringing total payments to bondholders to $2.1bn since Jan 2025. MoF says reserves took no undue strain. (Source: MoF/GBC) #GreaterNews #Ghana #GhanaEconomy

ACCRA IS FLOODING AGAIN — AND THIS TIME THE MILITARY IS OUT.

Torrential rain from Sunday night into Monday morning submerged major roads across the capital — the N1, the Kasoa highway, Weija, Kaneshie, Spintex, Atomic Junction and more.

Floodwater entered homes in several communities, forcing families to higher ground.

The Ghana Armed Forces have deployed under Operation Boafo, with the 48 Engineer Regiment stationed at strategic points.

It comes barely a week after the June 29 floods killed at least 13 people. If you are in a low-lying area: do not walk or drive through floodwaters.

(Source: Graphic Online | Modern Ghana | abcnewsgh)
#GreaterNews #Ghana #Accra #AccraFloods

Stay safe — and tell us: how is your area?

Accra flooded again after overnight torrential rain — N1, Kasoa highway, Weija, Kaneshie among areas submerged; homes flooded. The military has deployed under Operation Boafo. (Source: Graphic/Modern Ghana) #GreaterNews #Accra #AccraFloods
